Ad Text:
Pivotal role in a major transformation project
Shape the future of Supply Chain systems and process
Attractive remuneration package

This is a rare opportunity to lead a large-scale enterprise transformation, shaping Supply Chain design and implementation end-to-end. You’ll drive analysis and solution validation, identify process and system improvements, assess cross-functional impacts, and translate stakeholder needs into clear, actionable requirements.

Would you like to know more?

With over 70 years’ experience in the industry, Stratco is a dynamic, national leader in the manufacture and marketing of building and home improvement products. With an outstanding record of continual growth, Stratco has manufacturing plants and Home Improvement Stores in all mainland states of Australia and a presence in several international locations.

You’ll bring pace, ownership, and high standards—along with a sharp, solutions-focused mindset. In this role, you’ll lead and coach a team of SMEs, build strong stakeholder relationships, and take ownership of solution delivery—covering process design, requirements, testing, and implementation. You’ll also partner closely with the change management team to embed new ways of working.

Broader responsibilities will include;

Collaborate across all levels of the organisation to initiate and maintain effective engagement with the program.
Document processes, and manage requirements, identify opportunities for improvement, and guide toward our future state plan for the Supply Chain processes (Procurement, Inventory Management, Warehousing, Logistics and Dispatch, Production Planning and Production Execution).
Undertake all SME responsibilities and serve as a point of escalation for more complex or high-impact decisions.
Ensure delivered systems meet business requirements, align to agreed processes, and achieve a suitable level of quality and stability.

What you’ll bring

Accordingly, we are seeking an experienced senior BA and Team Lead with demonstrated experience in Supply Chain realms, accompanied with expertise in large ERP platforms (Oracle ERP preferred). You bring a strong track record in process improvement and enabling technology, ideally across full lifecycle implementations, along with:

Demonstrable experience in Supply Chain operations including Procurement, Inventory Management, Warehousing, Logistics and Dispatch, Production Planning and Production Execution
Experience in delivering process or technology change initiatives
Demonstrated experience leading SME functions or specialist teams within a complex environment
High integrity, professionalism, and a strong sense of accountability
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills across all levels of an organisation
Excellent problem-solving ability
Collaboration, with a strong commitment to knowledge‑sharing

If you’re a high-calibre leader who thrives on complexity and brings energy and to everything you do, this is your platform to stand out. You’ll be stepping into a visible, business-critical transformation where your thinking, influence, and delivery will genuinely shape outcomes.

In return, you’ll gain exposure to senior stakeholders, work alongside top-tier talent, and play a defining role in a transformation that matters—accelerating your career while leaving a lasting impact.

Life at Stratco includes 

Holistic Wellness & Mental Health Support | 24/7 EAP to team members & family | Flexible Working Arrangements | Team Member Discounts | Professional Development & Career Growth | Competitive Remuneration | Positive Workplace Culture & Inclusion    

 

Our EVP 

At Stratco, manufacturing is our foundation, but our people are our future. We empower our team to grow and succeed, within an inclusive and collaborative environment, where every individual matters, and innovation thrives. We are committed to offering fulfilling and rewarding career paths, ongoing learning and development, and recognition for outstanding service. Join us and build the future you’ve always envisaged, while redefining outdoor living and improving the lifestyles of our customers. 

At Stratco, Safety is paramount, and we take every measure to ensure our team members ‘Return Home Safe’. 

We value integrity, accountability, and respect. At Stratco, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace and welcome applications from all backgrounds.

Established in 1890, Boart Longyear is the world’s leading provider of innovative, safe, and productivity-driven drilling equipment and tooling. With a primary focus on mining and exploration activities spanning a wide range of commodities—including copper, gold, nickel, zinc, uranium and other metals and minerals—the Company also supports activities in the energy, oil sands exploration, and environmental sectors.

 

​Boart Longyear's Drilling Products division delivers advanced research and development capabilities and holds hundreds of patented designs to manufacture, market, and service reliable drill rigs, innovative drill string products, rugged performance tooling, durable drilling consumables, and high-quality parts for customers worldwide. These products are engineered to meet the evolving needs of the global drilling industry, offering maximum performance, reliability, and safety in the most demanding conditions.

 

The Demand Planning Manager owns the Demand and Market pillars of the divisional SIOP process. The role is accountable for developing an accurate, unbiased, and data-driven demand plan across all go-to-market regions, ensuring alignment with commercial strategy and financial objectives.

 

This position governs the demand forecasting process, demand analytics, CRM discipline, and performance management. It ensures optimal visibility of demand signals, risks, and opportunities while driving accountability across regional planning and commercial teams. The role acts as the integrator between Demand Planning, Commercial (Go-to-Market), Finance, Marketing, and Supply Planning to deliver a single aligned demand plan that underpins the division’s operating rhythm.

 

Want to know a little more about the role? Day-to-day responsibilities include:

·         Lead the Demand Review phase of the SIOP cycle, establishing governance, standards, and a consistent planning cadence across all regions

·         Develop and manage a single, consensus unconstrained demand plan by consolidating regional forecasts and market inputs

·         Provide leadership and oversight to regional demand planners, driving forecasting discipline, capability uplift, and alignment

·         Deliver advanced demand forecasting, analytics, and scenario modelling to identify risks, opportunities, and market trends

·         Integrate new product introductions, lifecycle changes, and segmentation strategies into demand plans

·         Own demand performance management, including forecast accuracy, bias, service metrics, and continuous improvement initiatives

·         Partner with Supply Planning to align demand signals with inventory strategies and optimise service, stock levels, and working capital

·         Govern CRM systems and sales pipeline processes, ensuring data integrity, visibility, and integration into demand planning

·         Align demand plans with financial forecasts, translating insights into revenue, margin, and growth implications

·         Act as the commercial voice within SIOP, enabling cross-functional alignment between sales, operations, and finance




A few other things we’re looking for:

·         Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain, Finance, Economics or related field (MBA and APICS CPIM/CSCP preferred)

·         7–12 years’ experience in demand planning, commercial planning, or SIOP/S&OP environments, with exposure to multi-region operations

·         Proven track record managing end-to-end demand planning processes and influencing senior commercial stakeholders in a matrixed organisation

·         Strong expertise in forecasting methodologies, demand analytics, and market modelling, with advanced analytical, statistical, and financial capability

·         Commercially astute with solid financial acumen and the ability to translate market signals into actionable demand plans

·         Highly effective stakeholder influencer, driving alignment across sales, marketing, operations, and finance

·         Data-driven mindset with strong focus on accuracy, accountability, and continuous improvement within a SIOP framework

·         Ability to balance growth ambitions with operational and supply constraints

·         Advanced systems capability across ERP/MRP and planning tools, including CRM systems (Salesforce, MS Dynamics, SAP, Oracle, PipeDrive), BI tools (Power BI, QlikView), and advanced planning platforms (Logility, Anaplan, Kinaxis, Demand Solutions)

 

In return, you will receive:

·         Opportunity to work as part of a truly global organisation, collaborating with teams across multiple regions and value streams

·         Play a key role in shaping and maturing our SIOP capability during a significant period of transformation

·         High visibility role with exposure to senior leadership and the opportunity to influence strategic decision-making

·         Work in a collaborative, cross-functional environment that partners closely with commercial, operations, and finance teams

·         Competitive remuneration package aligned to experience and impact

·         Access to our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for you and your immediate family

·         Access to a broad range of employee discounts, including health insurance, travel and lifestyle.

·         Career development opportunities within a global business with a strong focus on growth and continuous improvement

 

Our People

At Boart Longyear, people are our most important asset. We recognize that the best ideas emerge through collaboration among individuals with diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. That is why we are committed to fostering diverse, inclusive teams and work environments free from discrimination and harassment—spaces where everyone can contribute and be heard. 

 

Our Vision

To create products and solutions that empower the exploration and extraction of minerals to enable life and prosperity.

 

Our Values

Our success as a business depends on how we operate. The how is the distinctive way in which we conduct our business, guided by our values of:

·         Integrity

·         Health & Safety

·         Teamwork & Diversity

·         Customer Focus

·         Sustainability

 

At Boart Longyear, we innovate, we lead, and we grow together. Sound like your kind of team? Apply now. 

 

Respectfully, no recruitment agencies please.

This is an opportunity to step into a genuinely impactful security role within a high-performing, technology-driven environment. You will take ownership of security operations while shaping how security evolves as the business scales.

What you will be doing
You will lead the development, implementation and ongoing evolution of cyber security practices across the organisation. This includes owning and enhancing an established ISO 27001 aligned security management system and embedding security into day to day delivery.
  
Key areas of focus include:

Leading security monitoring, incident response and control design
Driving continuous improvement across security maturity and capability
Owning external audits and compliance across ISO 27001 and SOC 2

What sets this role apart
This role goes beyond execution. You will be trusted to think critically, challenge where needed and influence decisions that shape the organisation’s security posture.
  
You will also have the opportunity to:

You are an experienced security professional with a broad background across multiple domains such as threat detection, incident response, identity and access management and vulnerability management.

If you are looking for a role where you can combine technical depth, strategic thinking and real influence, this is worth a conversation.

Job Title: Technical Integration & Security Lead - Oracle EBS to Fusion Program
Ad Text:
The Technical Integration & Security Lead will drive the end-to-end business process workstream for an enterprise transformation migrating from Oracle EBS to Oracle Fusion Cloud (ERP, HCM, EPM).

This role is accountable for leading process discovery, challenging and improving current ways of working, and designing a fit-to-standard future state aligned to Oracle Fusion capabilities and organizational strategy.

This is not a "process scribe" role.
 
The successful candidate will be expected to facilitate critical assessment, identify process and control gaps, recommend pragmatic improvements, and partner with business and technical stakeholders to deliver an adoptable, compliant, and measurable future-state operating model.
 
We are looking for someone who is:

Strategic and pragmatic: improves outcomes without over-engineering.
Constructively challenging: questions legacy practices respectfully and effectively.
Systems thinking: understands upstream/downstream impacts across domains.
Outcome-driven: focuses on adoptable solutions and measurable benefits.
problem-solver: uses evidence, data, and clear decision frameworks
Essential requirements

Proven experience leading business process design for ERP transformations, ideally Oracle EBS to Oracle Fusion Cloud.
Strong understanding of Oracle ERP processes and capabilities (e.g., Financials, Procurement, Projects, Supply Chain—depending on scope).
High level deliverable knowledge & high level approach.
Exposure to or working knowledge of Oracle Fusion HCM and/or EPM and how they integrate end-to-end with ERP.
Demonstrated ability to critically assess, simplify, and improve processes—balancing business needs with standard platform capabilities.
Strong facilitation skills: able to run workshops, manage conflict, and guide stakeholders to decisions.
Ability to create clear, structured artefacts (process maps, decision logs, requirements, fit-gap analysis, RACI).
Excellent communication skills—able to translate between business, functional consultants, and technical teams.
The role would suit a high level BA that's moved into working on Oracle EBS to Fusion transformation projects - or a functional architect that is business process driven.

About Us

At Prevail, we believe success isn’t given, it’s built. We stand alongside discerning customers to protect what they’ve created in life and business, with the same care and commitment they put into building it.

We deliver crafted insurance solutions across niche segments: High Net Worth, Specialty (specialised small business), and Lifestyle & Leisure. These are customers with unique and complex needs, and we design coverage that fits, removes friction, and takes risk off their plate.

Everything we do aims at the best experience and the best outcome for the end customer: clear processes, fast decisions, reliable coverage, and measurable results. We act with integrity, own our outcomes, and get 1% better every day, progressive, energetic, and forward-thinking by design

About the Opportunity

Prevail is seeking a National Underwriting & Performance Manager a senior leadership role responsible for shaping and executing our underwriting strategy across High Net Worth, Lifestyle & Leisure, and Specialty portfolios. You will be the strategic underwriting face of Prevail, working alongside the CEO and Group Chief Actuarial.

You will be the strategic underwriting face of Prevail, both internally and externally. Working alongside the CEO, you will steer product mix, pricing strategy, underwriting frameworks and portfolio governance to deliver strong return on capital and sustainable growth for our insurer partners.

This role leads and develops our National Product Leads (currently three), providing clear direction, technical coaching and performance leadership. You’ll ensure our underwriting teams have the structure, capability, sophistication and agility needed to compete and win in a modern MGA environment.
 

Key Responsibilities

Lead Prevail’s national underwriting strategy across High Net Worth, Lifestyle & Leisure, and Specialty portfolios, ensuring strong alignment to insurer risk appetite and commercial objectives.
Drive portfolio performance and profitability using data-led insights to adjust pricing, risk selection, retention and distribution levers to maximise sustainable growth.
Act as the strategic underwriting face to insurer partners, building trust through transparent reporting, technical clarity and strong portfolio stewardship.
Provide senior technical oversight, making decisions on complex or non-standard risks and guiding Product Leads through high-value or unusual submissions.
Strengthen underwriting governance by maintaining consistent frameworks, guidelines, quality assurance and audit processes across all product lines.
Develop and uplift team capability through coaching, clear expectations and building future-ready underwriting structures that leverage digital enablement.

Skills and Qualifications

Significant experience in underwriting or insurance operations with proven leadership across complex or high-value portfolios.
Deep technical underwriting capability, including pricing fundamentals, risk assessment, portfolio management and strong commercial judgement.
Experience in high net worth or specialised personal lines, with confidence navigating bespoke or non-standard risks.
Strong stakeholder influence and communication skills for effective engagement with brokers, insurer partners and senior leadership.
Demonstrated ability to analyse portfolio trends, translate data into insights and convert findings into strategic actions.
A leadership style grounded in coaching, accountability and capability uplift, with experience supporting succession pathways.

Benefits

As a valued member of Prevail, we believe that when people thrive, performance follows. That’s why we’ve built a benefits offering that supports your wellbeing, growth, protection, and purpose—not just at work, but in life. We back our team with real flexibility, meaningful development, and a culture where accountability, integrity, and contribution matter

We believe in hybrid working, purposeful time together to build strong connections, spark ideas and move faster, while giving you the space and flexibility to focus and perform at your best.
You’ll grow through structured development, mentoring and tuition support, with ANZIIF accreditation pathways that convert learning into real capability.
You're backed by company-paid Life, TPD, Salary Continuance and Travel insurance, with access to additional discounted cover.
Your wellbeing is supported through annual health checks, skin checks, flu shots, holistic wellness programs and confidential counselling.
You’ll thrive in a collegiate, high-trust culture where integrity and accountability matter, your ideas land quickly, and your contribution counts.
 

Why Join Prevail

This is more than a role it’s your chance to help shape one of Australia’s most forward-thinking specialist underwriting agencies. At Prevail, you’ll work on complex, meaningful risks across High Net Worth, Specialty, Lifestyle & Leisure for customers who expect more and value precision.

You’ll be part of a high-performance, no-ego culture where ideas move fast, ownership is real, and the team succeeds together. We’re modernising how we work from better systems and digital platforms to refreshed, purpose-built office spaces designed to energise and connect.

And we back what matters to you with volunteer leave, a genuine focus on wellbeing, and a working rhythm that supports both your ambition and your life.

If you’re ready to grow with a business that’s moving fast, shape something meaningful, and help set a new benchmark for what great looks like in specialist insurance — this is your moment.

Our Culture & Values

At Prevail, our values aren’t just words  they’re behaviours we live every day. They guide how we work, how we show up for each other, and how we deliver results.

P – Problem-Solving We lean into challenges with creativity, energy, and action.

R – Relationships We build trust through genuine connection and shared success.

Senior Business Analyst (Contract) - Equipment Finance

Location: Sydney (Hybrid)
Contract Type: Day Rate Contract
Duration: 6-12 months (with potential extension)
Start Date: ASAP

About the Role
We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst with strong business-facing BA expertise to join a high-performing team delivering critical initiatives within the equipment finance domain. This is a hands-on contract role suited to someone who thrives in complex environments, engages confidently with senior stakeholders, and can translate business needs into clear, actionable outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end-to-end business analysis activities across multiple workstreams
Partner with business stakeholders to elicit, analyse, and document requirements
Facilitate workshops, interviews, and process mapping sessions
Translate business needs into functional specifications, user stories, and process flows
Drive process improvement initiatives and identify efficiency opportunities
Collaborate closely with technology teams to ensure accurate solution delivery
Support UAT planning, execution, and business readiness activities
Provide strategic input into business transformation initiatives

Key Requirements

Proven experience as a Senior Business Analyst in complex environments
Strong business BA skillset (not purely technical/delivery focused)
Demonstrated experience within equipment finance / asset finance / lending
Previous migration experience within financial services
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills (C-level exposure preferred)
Strong process mapping and documentation capabilities
Experience working across both Agile and Waterfall environments
Ability to operate autonomously and drive outcomes in a fast-paced setting

Desirable

Experience in financial services or banking
Familiarity with credit, leasing, or loan lifecycle processes
Exposure to regulatory or compliance-driven projects

What's on Offer

Competitive day rate
Flexible hybrid working environment
Opportunity to work on high-impact transformation programs
Collaborative and supportive team culture

How to Apply
If you are a seasoned Senior BA with strong business engagement skills and equipment finance experience, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now with your updated CV.




Only candidates with relevant equipment finance experience will be considered. Candidates must be based in Sydney with full work rights for consideration

Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.
Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.
For everyone, everywhere.

More about our mission and what we offer.

Job Description

Salary Range: 210,000 - 270,000 AUD per annum + RSUs + Super

What You’ll Do

Find your customers. Build an analytical, data-driven idea of what customers really want and iterate on it quickly. Do not just stop at what you think their users want - build what they actually will love to use. Dig into the data, run research, work with our design, engineering, data teams and more to really understand what the next big thing we’re going to build is.

Build it for your users. With product sense, conviction, and a lot of supporting data, expand or add on top of Wise’s award-winning app, website and APIs to deliver intuitive and groundbreaking experiences to customers

Make your product sustainable. Understand what value it provides to the user and price it in a fair way that allows you to operate it at the level of quality your users deserve, while running it sustainably for the years to com

Find your people. Build a large network inside the company that will let you get insights, ideas, critiques and help to ship world-class experiences within your team and with other teams.

Qualifications

What You’ll Bring

Experience in building consumer-facing products in Australia and New Zealand, or a strong affinity with high-growth products that delight users and would be recognised as category-defining. It does not need to be fintech or digital products: any experience in shipping things that customers love, is a must for this role. 

A clear vision of what led to successes or failures in your career. Products can be successful or not, what matters is what you learn from them

Analytical mindset: we don’t care about your academic background, but we deeply value the ability to use data to make informed decisions in highly ambiguous, fast-moving contexts. You will have plenty of analytics and experienced product support at your disposal, but you must be able to back your convictions and ideas with hard data to be able to run large-scale bets at Wise.

Nice to Have

Experience in a fintech or high growth finance-related company

A background in statistics/data analysis, or user research, or design/product design

SQL, Python, R or any data analysis software experience beyond Excel.

Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders  — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.
Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

12-month CONTRACT with potential extension

Hybrid working arrangement (1 day/week in Melbourne CBD)
Travel to Darwin, NT, required for one week each month

Travel expenses, accommodation, and meals will be provided

Daily rate: $900 - $1000 (inc SUPER)

ROLE OVERVIEW
We are looking for a Senior Change Analyst to partner closely with the Senior Change Manager, driving the tactical execution of strategic change programs and shaping how the business adapts to new ways of working. In this role, you will unpack complex enterprise system and process implementations to understand their true impact on the workforce, blending traditional change management, project analysis, and HR business partnering.

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Design, schedule, and roll out structured change management plans alongside the Senior Change Manager, championing the use of standardized change tools and templates for consistent delivery.
Drive comprehensive change impact assessments to uncover how system and process shifts will affect people and behaviors.
Track transition readiness and proactively escalate risks, dependencies, or roadblocks to program leadership.
Map out complex stakeholder landscapes and craft compelling, clear communication materials to boost awareness, understanding, and ultimate adoption.
Work hand-in-hand with the Learning & Development team to pinpoint capability gaps, manage training logistics, track attendance, and evaluate post-training feedback.
Own the maintenance of crucial change artifacts and registers, providing transparent, data-driven reporting on adoption metrics, readiness status, and cumulative change impacts.
Act as the connective layer between project delivery teams, People & Culture, and internal communications, sharing insights and lessons learned to upskill the broader team.

MORE ABOUT YOU

Previous exposure to driving business or system transformations within the Utilities sector.

Demonstrate a proven track record of supporting end-to-end change management activities across business and technology-enabled transformations, including process overhauls and system rollouts.

Apply structured change methodologies, excelling at tracking dependencies, managing action items, and maintaining project artifacts.

Bring an HR Business Partnering mindset to change, skilfully assessing role impacts, coaching leaders through transitions, and aligning workforce readiness with strategic business goals.

Utilize top-tier active listening skills and the ability to translate highly complex technical concepts into clear, practical communications for diverse audiences across all levels.

Thrive as a resilient problem solver in fast-paced, ambiguous environments, remaining highly organized and capable of conducting root-cause analysis to unblock issues.

Build strong, positive relationships effortlessly, using influence rather than authority to unite project, business, and change teams around shared outcomes.


If this opportunity aligns with your expertise and aspirations, please apply! Please feel free to reach out directly to Muneem Meah at muneem.meah(at)aurec.com, should you have any questions!


Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted!

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

As SEEK’s Strategy Principal, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering our long-term commercial vision. Reporting to the APAC Director of Strategy, you’ll lead cross-functional projects that shape SEEK’s marketplace strategy across Asia-Pacific. You’ll guide teams, influence stakeholders and turn complex data into clear insights that drive executive decision-making. With a strong focus on collaboration, you’ll bridge strategy and execution — ensuring SEEK’s bold ambitions translate into meaningful outcomes for our customers, partners and community.

You will:

own delivery of commercial strategy development from framing problems to presenting solutions;
collaborate across Product, Commercial, AI, Finance, Marketing and Country Leadership teams;
build deep expertise in market trends, customer needs and competitive dynamics;
communicate strategy clearly and effectively at all levels of SEEK;
coach and mentor team members to grow capability and performance;
drive continuous improvement in strategy operating models and ways of working; and
represent Commercial Strategy in leadership forums and strategic initiatives.

Key responsibilities

Lead strategic projects that shape SEEK’s long-term vision.
Frame problems, analyse data and build actionable insights.
Partner with business leaders to prioritise and execute strategic initiatives.
Simplify complex narratives into clear recommendations for executives.
Mentor and develop team members for high performance.
Lead cross-functional collaboration across Product, Commercial and regional teams.
Track market trends and translate insights into thought leadership.

The Team

SEEK global Strategy team is composed of ~30 mostly ex-consultants from top-tier consulting firms. We offer an intellectually stimulating environment, a great team with a caring heart, a fun and inspiring environment and a great purpose to wake up to every morning. You will work closely and interact on a regular basis with:

Director of Strategy, Commercial
Director of Strategy, Product
Group Executive, Commercial
Group Executive, Product
Directors of the Commercial Leadership team
Other members of the Strategy Leadership Team
Other members of the Executive team on an ad-hoc basis
Other members of the APAC SEEK strategy team
Qualifications
8+ years' experience in strategy roles, including 5+ years in top-tier consulting, PE or investment banking.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proven success leading strategic projects from concept to delivery.
Experience influencing senior stakeholders across functions and markets.
Demonstrated people management and coaching experience.
Deep interest in technology, online marketplaces and innovation cultures.
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Free income protection insurance

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Job Title: Program Manager - Growth and Operational Excellence
Ad Text:
Company Description

Ramsay provides high quality healthcare to millions of people each year through a network of hospitals, clinics and services in Australia, Europe and the United Kingdom. 

Job Description

Program Manager – Growth & Operational Excellence 

This role can be based in Sydney (St Leonards), Melbourne (St Kilda) or Brisbane, but a flexible approach to WFH is supported. 

 

Job Summary:

You will report to the Director, Growth & Operational Excellence, this pivotal role offers an exceptional opportunity for a Program Manager to serve as a key strategic partner within our Growth & Operational Excellence team.

You will support the delivery of high-impact, data-driven performance improvement initiatives, ensuring initiatives align seamlessly with our strategic objectives, resolving roadblocks, and keeping the team on track.

This role requires a proactive leader who can anticipate challenges, facilitate executive-level collaboration, and translate strategic vision into actionable programs.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Program Leadership: Coordinate and facilitate the design and implementation of multiple programs of work, ensuring direct alignment with our strategic objectives and delivering tangible, high-impact outcomes across the organisation. This includes planning and leading detailed quarterly planning sessions, preparing for Quarterly Business Reviews, and ensuring structures and ways of working are set up to deliver on planned outcomes.

Strategic Reporting & Insights: Design and prepare executive-level presentations covering program progress, outcomes, and decisions required to senior executive management, providing data-driven recommendations that inform strategic decision-making.

Strategic Communication & Stakeholder Engagement: Cultivate clear, concise, and impactful communication across all levels, from team members to site executive leadership to the Australian senior executive team. You will drive consensus and foster productive relationships, acting as a critical interface, translating executive priorities into a clear program plan and facilitating seamless cross-functional collaboration.

Proactive Risk Mitigation: Anticipate potential program obstacles and proactively develop robust contingency plans, ensuring the smooth progression and successful delivery of initiatives.

Performance Insight & Optimisation: Maintain and improve on existing frameworks to monitor program effectiveness and delivery. You will proactively identify areas for improvement, guiding necessary adjustments and resolutions.

Budget & Resource Stewardship: Develop and manage program budgets, including forecasting, tracking expenses, and strategically reallocating resources to maximise program effectiveness and efficiency.

 

Qualifications:

Education: A Bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Management, Business Administration, or a related field is essential.

Experience: A minimum of 5 years of demonstrated experience in program management, strategic consulting, or a related role, with a proven track record of delivering complex, high-impact projects. Experience related to the health sector is preferred.

Skills: Exceptional leadership, strategic communication, and organisational skills. Advanced proficiency in data analysis, business intelligence tools, and project/program management software. Ability to influence without direct authority.

 

Personal Attributes:

Strategic & Results-Oriented: Possesses a keen strategic mindset with an unwavering focus on delivering high-quality, measurable outcomes that advance organisational goals.

Adaptable & Resilient: Thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, demonstrating exceptional adaptability and resilience when navigating complex challenges.

Proactive Problem Solver: A proactive approach to identifying challenges and an innate ability to develop practical, innovative solutions.

Trusted Advisor: Builds strong relationships based on trust and credibility, acting as a reliable advisor to senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

High Integrity & Professionalism: Upholds the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct.

 

About the Growth & Operational Excellence Team

Ramsay’s Growth & Operational Excellence team has built a strong reputation across the business for identifying and implementing practical, high-impact, and data-driven performance improvements through close collaboration with Operations Executive Teams. We combine an extensive range of skills and experience from both within and outside the business, and we pride ourselves on being dynamic, constructive, and collaborative.

This opportunity is ideal for a motivated and experienced Program Manager ready to help address the most pressing operational challenges and opportunities in the business. We are seeking someone who is passionate about using their strategic problem-solving acumen, analytical mindset, and strong relationship-building toolkit to support our hospitals to deliver high-value healthcare.

 

What’s in it for You:

At Ramsay, we care for our people by offering benefits that truly support your career, wellbeing and lifestyle, including:

Grow your career with recognition programs, employee referral bonuses, scholarships, free access to LinkedIn Learning and the Ramsay Leadership Academy
Save more every day with Ramsay Rewards, giving you exclusive deals at 100+ retailers, restaurants, travel providers and more
Enjoy peace of mind with lower hospital cover costs and higher extras limits on services like General and Major Dental, plus pharmacy and allied health discounts
Find balance through flexible rostering options, flexible leave and access to free, confidential counselling and wellbeing support 24/7
Increase your take-home pay through salary packaging and novated leasing, plus discounts on leisure travel, gyms and lifestyle services.

 

Why Join Ramsay:

At Ramsay Health Care, “people caring for people” is at the heart of everything we do. We are:

Australia’s largest private hospital operator, with over 74 sites and 35,000 staff
A values-led workplace where you’ll be supported personally and professionally
A place with flexible, permanent and career-growth opportunities nationwide.
Additional Information

You must provide or complete a National Police Check conducted within the previous 12 months.

Apply:

Ready to feel cared for while you care for others? Apply today. All applications must be lodged online.

Applications close: 28 April 2026

Reference number: JR111713

Please Note:

Ramsay Health Care is committed to Child Safety. Details of our commitment are set out in our Code of Conduct, available at www.ramsayhealth.com.au.

Applications made by recruitment agencies will not be considered.

We are seeking a Senior Data Architect for a fixed 3-month contract with deep, hands-on experience designing and delivering Medallion Architecture and Customer 360 platforms. This is a technically demanding role that requires someone who has personally built unified customer data models, identity resolution layers and production-grade lakehouse solutions at enterprise scale, not simply designed them on paper. 




Responsibilities:

Design the architecture and hands-on implementation of a Medallion architecture 
Design source-to-target mappings, data models, transformation logic and business rules for each layer of the medallion architecture. 
Implement identity resolution and crosswalk logic that links customer identifiers across different systems. 
Define semantic-layer business logic, governed metrics and KPI definitions for downstream analytics consumption. 
Productionize the end-to-end solution with data-quality checks, monitoring, orchestration and documentation. 
Partner with business stakeholders across marketing, operations, product and technology to translate requirements into technical delivery. 
Provide build ready requirements and artifacts to data engineers and analysts on medallion best practices, identity resolution patterns and data quality standards. 



Experience:

Proven delivery of enterprise-scale Medallion Architecture, including Silver and Gold data models for business-ready analytics
Hands-on experience taking data platforms from development to production, with strong quality, monitoring and operational standards
Direct experience building customer identity resolution / crosswalk solutions, including deterministic and probabilistic matching
Track record delivering a Customer 360 / single customer view, integrating CRM, digital, contact-centre and transactional data
Strong capability in customer-centric data modelling, semantic layers and governed metrics as a single source of truth
Experience modelling digital, behavioural and contact-centre data (e.g. GA4, Adobe, Genesys or similar)
Advanced skills in Python, SQL and modern lakehouse platforms (Microsoft Fabric, Databricks or equivalent)
Experience building automated, production-grade data pipelines with orchestration, data quality and governance controls
Expertise in Power BI semantic models and executive reporting
Desirable: experience in insurance or financial services, customer analytics or NLP/text analytics use cases

Company description:


Shape growth strategy, new ventures and AI-enabled investment decisions across Kleenheat and WesCEF
Build a long-term career with WesCEF — competitive salary, share allocations, and opportunities across the Wesfarmers Group
Permanent, full-time role based at our new Perth CBD office.

Kleenheat is a trusted Western Australian energy brand with more than 65 years of history, supplying natural gas and electricity to homes and businesses across WA. As part of WesCEF and the broader Wesfarmers Group, Kleenheat is well positioned for future growth across energy retail, solar, batteries and emerging technologies.

We are seeking a Senior Commercial Analyst to play a high‑impact role across Kleenheat's Natural Gas & Electricity (NG&E) business and AI‑enabled initiatives across WesCEF.

This is an opportunity for a commercially minded analyst who wants exposure to growth initiatives, new ventures and enterprise‑scale decision making, while building a long‑term career within one of Australia's most successful diversified groups.

We pride ourselves on our values of One Team, Accountable, Customer Focused and Forward Thinking, with the understanding that our success is underpinned by our people.



Job description:



About the role

As a Senior Commercial Analyst, you will play a high‑impact role across Kleenheat's Natural Gas & Electricity business and AI‑enabled initiatives across WesCEF. You will support strategic growth initiatives, new ventures and emerging energy solutions, providing the commercial insight and rigour that underpins high‑value investment and prioritisation decisions.

Partnering closely with stakeholders across Commercial, Business Development, Digital and IT, you will translate complex modelling, customer economics and AI use cases into clear actionable insights. You will influence investment decisions, optimise growth initiatives and provide insight on commercial performance, risks and trade-offs.

Key Responsibilities:

Growth & strategic initiatives

Support and shape growth initiatives, including digital, AI‑enabled, and distributed energy solutions.
Provide commercial support to Kleenheat Spark ventures, from opportunity assessment through to performance tracking.
Translate emerging WesCEF AI use cases into tangible commercial value, focusing on revenue uplift, cost efficiency, and customer outcomes.

Commercial analytics & insights

Develop and own commercial models, forecasts, and business cases to support investment and prioritisation decisions.
Analyse customer behaviour, pricing, offers, and lifecycle value to inform growth and retention strategies.
Identify risks and opportunities across growth initiatives and provide clear, actionable recommendations to stakeholders.

Stakeholder engagement

Partner with teams across Commercial, Business Development, Digital, and IT within Kleenheat and the broader WesCEF group.
Present insights and recommendations to senior leadership in a clear, commercially focused way.
Act as a trusted advisor on commercial performance, trade‑offs, and growth levers.

About You

Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Commerce, Economics, Finance (or a closely related discipline)
Strong experience in commercial analysis, strategy, or business analytics, ideally in energy, utilities, or complex consumer environments.
Demonstrated ability to support new products, ventures, or growth initiatives from concept through to execution.
Strong financial and commercial modelling capability (business cases, ROI, scenario analysis).
Comfortable working with large datasets, dashboards, and analytics tools (e.g. Power BI).
Curiosity and capability to work with AI‑enabled insights and emerging digital platforms.
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the confidence to challenge and influence.
Comfortable operating in ambiguous, fast‑moving environments typical of innovation and growth initiatives.
Clear communicator who can simplify complexity for non‑technical audiences.

What we offer

At Wesfarmers Chemicals, Energy & Fertilisers (WesCEF), we value our people and offer a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, development and career growth, including:

Competitive remuneration, including access to the Wesfarmers employee share plan and participation in our annual incentive plan
Flexible working arrangements, including some work-from-home options where applicable
Salary sacrifice opportunities, including novated leasing
Access to a wide range of employee benefits across the Wesfarmers Group, including staff discounts at Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks and Kleenheat, as well as access to Wesfarmers-sponsored arts and cultural events
Paid parental leave of up to 18 weeks
Ongoing professional development and career opportunities across WesCEF and the broader Wesfarmers Group

Why WesCEF

At WesCEF, we are committed to building an engaged, high-performing and inclusive workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate. We value diversity of thinking, skills and experience, knowing this drives innovation and better business outcomes.

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples to apply.

We are proud to support flexible working arrangements and understand that flexibility looks different for everyone. If flexibility is important to you, we encourage you to discuss your needs as part of the application process.

WesCEF operates across multiple locations, with our two major hubs based in Kwinana and the Perth CBD.

Applications close: Sunday 3rd May 2026

Our recruitment process considers your alignment to our company values and the requirements of the role. We also have several checks which may be conducted to demonstrate your suitability for the role including but not limited to psychometric assessment, police checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, right to work check, qualifications, due diligence and/or reference checks.    

We reserve the right to commence the recruitment process prior to the closing date. Therefore, it is recommended to apply as soon as possible.    

Agency applications will not be accepted.

Head of Data & AI | Sydney | ASX Listed Digital Financial Services

The opportunity
A high growth, ASX listed digital financial services business is looking for a Head of Data & AI to lead its next chapter of innovation across agentic AI, machine learning, and enterprise data strategy.

This is a rare leadership role at the intersection of AI vision and execution. You'll own the end-to-end data and AI strategy, from data foundations and advanced analytics through to generative AI and agentic systems.

What makes this exciting
This isn't a "build the strategy and hand it off" role. You'll lead high performing engineering teams, stand up agentic infrastructure, drive AI into products and operations, and shape what the next generation hybrid workforce looks like in a regulated financial services environment. The business is profitable, ambitious, and ready to move fast.

You will have a genuine mandate to industrialise AI across the enterprise not just experiment with it.

What you'll do
Define and execute the Data & AI strategy across engineering, ML platforms, and agentic operations. Build and scale AI powered products and services for customers and merchants. Are you the person in the room who actually knows how a profit centre ticks — and can translate that into a system requirement?

We're looking for a senior Business Systems Analyst who has walked the floor of an electrical trade or wholesale business, understands the systems that make it run, and can bridge the gap between operations and IT.

The Company

MM Electrical (MMEM) is one of Australia's largest electrical wholesale and trade retail networks. Our Business Systems and Support team keeps the technology engine running — from ERP to EDI to integrations — across a national network of branches and profit centres.

The Role

This is a newly created senior position that sits at the intersection of L2 support and business analysis — a genuine 50/50 split. You'll report directly to the Head of Business Systems and Support and work alongside a tight-knit, highly capable team including our senior BA, L1 and L2 support teams and in-house developer.

There's no hand-holding here — we want someone who can pick up a complex support ticket and drive it to resolution in the morning, then sit in a requirements workshop with the Finance team in the afternoon.

What You'll Be Doing

• Business Analysis: requirements elicitation, process mapping, solution design, and translating business needs into clear specs for our developer.

• L2 Support: owning escalated issues across Unibis ERP, EDI (B2BE), and integrated finance and reporting systems.

• Working with our in-house developer to test and deliver system enhancements.

• Engaging Finance, operations, and branch stakeholders to gather insights and drive improvements.

• Contributing to knowledge base documentation and the continuous improvement of our support capability.

What We're Looking For

• 5+ years in a Business Systems Analyst, IT Systems Analyst, or equivalent role — you've done this before.

• Real-world experience inside an electrical trade retail or wholesale business — think Middy's, Rexel, Voltex, or similar.

• You know how a profit centre operates end-to-end: POS, purchasing, inventory, branch accounting, customer accounts.

• You can write a proper requirements document AND work a support ticket through to resolution — both with equal professionalism.

• Unibis ERP experience is a genuine advantage, but if you have the domain knowledge and the BA skills, we'll teach you the platform.

• EDI experience (B2BE or equivalent) is a bonus.

• You're autonomous, comfortable working hybrid, and you communicate clearly with everyone from the branch manager to the CFO.

The Details

• Salary: $130,000 – $160,000 + superannuation (depending on experience)

• Working arrangement: Hybrid — Currently 3 days North Rocks office / 2 days WFH

• Employment type: Permanent, full-time

• Location: North Rocks, NSW (must be within reasonable commute)

• Australian work rights are required for this position.

If you've spent years working with electrical trade systems and you're ready to bring that knowledge into a head office IT role with real scope, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply now with your CV and a Cover Letter outlining your experience within the electrical trade industry and the business sytems you've worked with.

Applicants who fail to submit a cover letter will not be considered for this role.

Map data flows and interface specifications, as well as define technical requirements (with support from the team’s solution architecture stream).

Document current-state technical architectures to inform the migration strategies for the program.

The chance to play a key role in a long term ICT modernisation project

About Us

Praemium was established in 2001 to simplify and streamline the complexities of wealth management. Today, we offer a fully integrated platform that enables every part of an investor’s wealth to be managed in one place.

Managing over $67 billion in funds under administration for leading institutions, private wealth firms and financial advisers, we deliver innovative technology and exceptional service to our clients.

We are committed to empowering better business decisions through data, insights and technology. Join us as we continue to redefine wealth management in Australia.

 

The Role

We are seeking a Corporate Reporting Analyst to join our Corporate Operations team.

This role is responsible for designing, building and continuously improving corporate dashboards and reporting that drive business decision-making and operational transparency across the organisation.

With a strong focus on Power BI and Microsoft Fabric, you will translate business questions into clear, trusted and well-governed reporting solutions.

Beyond building dashboards, you will play a key role in uplifting data literacy across the business — helping stakeholders interpret insights, build confidence in data, and move towards self-service analytics.

This is a highly collaborative role suited to someone who enjoys combining technical capability with stakeholder engagement and commercial thinking.

 

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop and maintain Power BI dashboards supporting executive, operational and governance reporting
Build and enhance reporting solutions using Microsoft Fabric, including governed datasets and semantic models
Translate business requirements into clear, accurate and visually compelling dashboards
Partner with stakeholders across Finance, Operations, Risk and Corporate teams to understand reporting needs
Act as a trusted analytics partner, delivering insights and recommendations (not just reports)
Educate stakeholders on interpreting data, metrics and trends to improve data literacy
Develop and maintain supplier and vendor performance dashboards (SLA/KPI reporting)
Analyse supplier performance trends and provide insights for governance and decision-making
Use SQL to query, validate and analyse data across enterprise systems
Investigate data issues and partner with system owners to improve data quality
Support the adoption of self-service analytics and reduce manual reporting

About You

You are a detail-oriented and commercially minded reporting professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You are equally comfortable building dashboards and engaging with stakeholders to influence better decisions.

You will have:

Strong experience in corporate or enterprise reporting environments
Advanced Power BI dashboard development capability
Experience with Microsoft Fabric or modern Microsoft data platforms
Working knowledge of SQL for data analysis and validation
Proven ability to translate business questions into actionable insights
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain data to non-technical audiences
High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Experience supporting data literacy or analytics enablement initiatives

Qualifications:

Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) – required
Microsoft Certified: Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate (DP-600) – desirable
Exposure to financial services or regulated environments – advantageous

 What We Offer

Flexible hybrid work environment
Competitive salary package
Comprehensive leave benefits, including paid parental leave
Access to financial wellbeing initiatives and novated leasing
Ongoing professional development and certification support
Modern office with end of trip facilities located on Collins Street, Melbourne

 

How to Apply

Please apply with your CV and a cover letter outlining your experience and alignment to the role.

We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome applications from all qualified candidates.

Please note: Applicants must have full, unrestricted working rights in Australia (PR or citizenship).

A police check will be required for successful candidates.

Job Title
Associate Director Wholesale Banking Analyst Job Description


About us

Rabobank is the world’s leading specialist in food and agribusiness banking. In Australia and New Zealand, our Wholesale Banking franchise is a core growth platform, supporting large and complex corporate and institutional clients across food & agribusiness, commodities and the energy transition. Our success is built on deep sector knowledge, long-standing client relationships and a strong risk culture. We are a business that continues to invest and grow, and our people are central to that journey

About the Role

Due to ongoing growth in our Wholesale Banking business across Australia and New Zealand, we are seeking an Associate Director to join our Portfolio Management team. This is a front-line credit role, embedded in deal teams, where you will partner closely with Relationship Managers, Product Specialists and Risk to support origination, structuring and ongoing management of complex lending transactions.

In this role, you will play a critical part in enabling the bank’s strategic growth ambitions while maintaining a disciplined and robust risk framework. You will have exposure to a high-quality, diversified wholesale portfolio and the opportunity to contribute to a collaborative, high-performing team within an established and expanding franchise.

Key Responsibilities: 

Credit Application Preparation & Risk Assessment

Prepare clear, concise and high-quality credit submissions in line with Rabobank’s credit risk policies, underwriting standards and risk appetite Analyse business, non-financial and sustainability risks, including sector dynamics

Undertake detailed financial analysis, including historical performance, forecasts and sensitivities

Assess group structures, legal entities and counterparty risk

Determine and support key credit risk parameters such as PD and LGD

Client Monitoring & Portfolio Management

Complete annual client credit reviews and ongoing portfolio monitoring, including covenant compliance and special conditions

Proactively identify and respond to emerging client or portfolio risks Contribute to portfolio insights, reviews and continuous improvement initiatives

To be successful you will have:

Qualifications and Experience

Degree in Finance, Accounting or a related discipline; postgraduate qualification desirable

Minimum 5 years’ experience in a wholesale or institutional banking environment, within credit analysis, credit approval or portfolio management

Skills & Capabilities

Strong credit and risk assessment capability with sound commercial judgement

High-quality financial analysis and structured problem-solving skills

Confident written and verbal communicator, comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders

 Strong relationship-building skills and a collaborative, team-oriented mindset

 Proactive, detail-oriented and able to manage priorities in a deadline-driven environment

What we are proud to offer you:

A competitive remuneration package will be offered for the successful candidate.

​Wellbeing leave. These 2 extra leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives.

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development.

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (Primary carer up-to 14 weeks paid leave)

Bonus and Additional Leave. We realise sometimes 4 weeks Annual Leave is not enough! At Rabobank we provide an incentive for eligible employees to receive 1 extra week Annual Leave and an option to purchase another 2 weeks Annual Leave.

Extra Insurance. Rabobank recognises that employees need to protect their financial wellbeing, in the event of serious illness, injury, or even death. Rabobank provides eligible permanent employees with employer-funded Death, TPD and Income Protection Insurance.

Rabo Workplace Giving program. As part of Rabobank's global Corporate Social Responsibility, Workplace Giving aims to encourage employees to contribute to our community. Rabo Workplace Giving program matches employee donations to selected Social Partners.

Our Values

Rabobank Australia values inclusion, belonging, and positive experiences for all. Our work environment, our benefits, and the way we live our values, “Client Driven,” “Responsible,” “Professional” and “Cooperative” make it a great place to work. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds.

Please let our Talent Acquisition team know if you need any accommodations to make our opportunities more accessible to you.

Rabobank is not accepting submissions from recruitment agencies for this role.

You must hold valid work rights to be considered for this role.

Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work
Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

About the Role

Lead and contribute to the Data Platform strategy and roadmap. Working alongside engineering leadership, you'll shape the vision for how Canva's data platform evolves — prioritising investments across ingestion, transformation, orchestration, governance, and the Semantic Layer. You'll build the business cases that unlock resources and keep the platform aligned to company goals.

Chart the course for the AI first evolution of data, analytics and decision making. You'll define how Canva leverages AI to transform analytics, insights, and decision-making. This is early-stage work that needs product leadership to move from experimentation to a coherent, scalable strategy — including conversational analytics, automated decisioning, and AI-powered self-serve insights.

Build deep relationships across a complex stakeholder landscape. Your users span data scientists, analytics engineers, business teams, and company leadership. You'll maintain regular touchpoints, run forums, and build the trust needed to align competing priorities across a broad surface area.

Drive platform adoption, enablement, and measurable impact. You'll establish the measurement frameworks that track platform health, adoption, and ROI. You'll drive enablement programs that help teams self-serve, and tell the story of DPG's impact through metrics, case studies, and regular stakeholder updates.

Strengthen governance, access, and data trust. You'll ensure the platform's governance and access controls mature alongside Canva — driving improvements to data sharing, retention, security, and audit readiness in partnership with engineering, security, and legal.

You're probably a match if

This is a specialised domain role. We're looking for someone with deep experience in data platforms and a track record of leading product in technically complex, high-stakes environments.

You've managed major data platforms for significant digital organisations. You understand the full stack - from ingestion and transformation through to BI and analytics - and you're fluent in how data science and AI teams consume platform capabilities.

You've worked with Snowflake, Google Cloud/BigQuery, AWS, Databricks or equivalent platforms at scale. You understand the architecture trade-offs and can hold your own in technical conversations.

 You've led major shifts in data platform architecture and strategic direction — not just incremental improvements, but meaningful changes to how an organisation manages, governs, and leverages its data.

You've worked on or shaped AI capabilities such as personalisation, conversational analytics, automated decisioning, agentic workflows or AI-driven data products. You know what's hype and what's real.

You've navigated stakeholder groups spanning engineering, data science, product, finance, marketing, and go-to-market. You know how to build alignment across competing priorities and earn trust with deeply technical teams.

Experience with analytics and visualisation platforms (Tableau, Looker, Mode, Omni, or open-source tools), or experience navigating regulatory, compliance, or enterprise data governance requirements at scale.

About the team

The Infra SuperGroup at Canva makes complex technology simple for Canvanauts by building foundational platforms that propel Canva's growth. Every week, thousands of engineers interact with the tools, utilities, and platforms that Infra provides. Infra plays a direct role in improving developer impact across the entire software lifecycle.

Within Infra, the Data Platform Group (DPG) builds and operates the data platform so Canvanauts can self-serve reliable, governed, and performant data. DPG provides paved-road capabilities — tools, patterns, and SLO-backed services — while domain teams own their data products. The group's surface area spans ingestion, transformation, orchestration, governance, observability, activation, and BI/analytics. It's built on Snowflake, dbt, Apache Airflow, and a growing governed Semantic Layer that powers both BI and emerging AI use cases.

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

Introduction

As a member the Promontory Australia team at IBM you will contribute to the firm's efforts in working with clients on challenging strategic, risk management, and regulatory compliance issues.

Our team of specialists offers the following services across Prudential, Conduct and AML Regulation:

· Risk Management – Providing advisory and assurance services regarding organisational transformation across risk management, governance, culture, accountability and compliance.

· Remediation Services – Assurance to regulators and firms over customer focused remediation programs.

· Regulatory Framework – Advising regulators and industry associations on building regulatory frameworks.

· Regulatory Liaison – Helping our clients navigate relationships with regulators.

· RegTech – Working with IBM teams and leveraging their technology assets in advising clients on AI based solutions and associated risk governance.

We have a diverse client base in Australia, which includes some of Australia's largest financial institutions and clients drawn from the public sector.

As a Principal, you will be responsible for managing risk, regulatory and compliance engagements on a day-to-day basis, analysing complex problems, developing recommendations to address client issues and heavily involved in reports and other deliverables. A strong consulting background with experience in non-financial risk management (including operational risk and resilience) and or superannuation is preferable. You will also be expected to draw upon your insights and networks to identify business opportunities for IBM Promontory to pursue.




Roles and Responsibilities

To be successful in this role a candidate must possess strong leadership skills and have the stature to engage effectively with clients and potential clients at all levels. The candidate will also need to inspire and work effectively with team members drawn from a range of technical disciplines. Interact comfortably with executive management across industry and regulatory authorities. Key responsibilities will include:

Manage projects and act as client point of contact on a day-to-day basis contributing to engagements being delivered to budget and meeting profit and realisation targets.

Undertake analysis as directed and take responsibility for the preparation of project deliverables, including reports and presentations.

Interact comfortably with executive management of and regulatory authorities, generally with a Managing Director present.

Strong analytical skills, oral skills, with demonstrated ability to communicate clearly, simply and logically.

Strong written communication skills with, the ability to construct well-founded, clear, and concise analyses and recommendations.

Ability to handle complex information, solve problems, and manage multiple tasks.

Ability to work closely with, and communicate effectively with, clients and within all levels of the organisation.

Strong commitment to integrity and ethics.

The candidate will also be required to build and retain relationships across the industry and to identify opportunities for further work. This includes the ability to establish and maintain positive relationships and build teams that are motivated to succeed.




Required Professional and Technical Expertise

· An undergraduate degree in either Accounting, Commerce, Finance, or Law - with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· At least 6 years experience in Financial services, Consulting, Regulator, Compliance, Risk management, Governance or legal.

· Track record working in a fast-paced and unstructured environment with aptitude for identifying and developing innovative solutions to client challenges.

· Analytics and data: Proven track record of successfully delivering highly analytical and data intensive work. Strong understanding of data governance and experience in working with large and unstructured datasets to draw meaningful and actionable insights and outcomes.

· AI: A strong understanding of AI fundamentals, including AI governance, and model development and application.

· Expert level capabilities in Excel (incl. advanced excel functionality and VBA / Macro development). Expert level capabilities in working with large datasets in SQL.




Preferred Professional and Technical Expertise

· Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance, Law or Business Administration with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· Specialising in data analytics, risk management, assurance, and regulatory compliance.

· Experience developing, testing and validating the end-to-end modelling process and knowledge of a variety of tools including Alteryx, R and Python.




About the Business Unit:

IBM Promontory, a business unit of IBM Consulting, is the premier consulting firm for financial services companies. IBM Promontory excels at helping clients resolve critical issues, particularly those with a regulatory dimension. Promontory professionals have unparalleled regulatory experience and insight, and provide our clients with frank, proactive advice informed by best practices and regulatory expectations.

Promontory Australia is a boutique operation and maintains links to the US and UK. Our consultants in Australia have diverse backgrounds from regulatory authorities, industry and consulting.

In recent years Promontory has completed a number of high profile engagements with Australian financial institutions and due to a strong pipeline of work we are looking to expand our team with a highly skilled, adaptable and dependable individual to join the high performing Sydney office.

About the Group/Team

Canva's AI Platform Group serves as the foundation for AI innovation across the company, and achieving our GenAI ambitions depends on its ability to scale effectively. Within this group, the AI Enablement teams provide critical solution expertise to researchers and AI builders globally, helping them navigate platform capabilities, apply expert domain knowledge, optimise implementations, and accelerate the journey from experimentation to production. These teams bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and reliable, production-ready features, ensuring that innovation translates into real user value.

About the Role/Specialty

Please note, this is not a model building role

This role sits at the intersection of solution expertise and platform engineering, requiring deep technical expertise across ML domains combined with strong platform knowledge. The senior MLE will context-switch between facilitating model evaluations, data pipeline, optimising inference performance and working directly with product teams building solutions.

The nature of enablement work demands someone who operates as a force multiplier, finding satisfaction not just in solving technical problems but in enabling others to move faster. The role operates in a high-velocity environment where priorities shift rapidly based on team needs, requiring both technical excellence and pragmatic judgment. This combination of deep expertise and adaptive problem-solving is critical to accelerating the delivery of GenAI capabilities across Canva, removing friction from the path to production and ensuring product teams across the company can ship AI-powered features at the pace and quality our ambitions require.

What you’ll do (responsibilities):


Responsibilities will evolve as Canva's needs change, but here's what the role will focus on initially:

Act as a solution expert across ML domains including evaluations, training, inference, data pipelines, labelling, and optimisation

Work directly alongside product teams as a trusted partner, helping them navigate technical challenges and arrive at effective solutions

Provide expert guidance on platform capabilities, helping teams understand what's possible and how to get there efficiently

Develop blueprints, patterns, and paved roads that allow other teams to follow proven approaches and accelerate their own implementations

Debug and resolve complex issues, identifying root causes and sharing learnings broadly

Balance hands-on problem solving with strategic thinking about how to scale enablement impact across the organisation

You're probably a match if you have any of the following:

Strong Python expertise with experience in ML frameworks

Experience working with Kubernetes

Experience or knowledge of building data pipelines

Experience or knowledge of model evaluations

Experience with inference optimisation and serving infrastructure

Experience supporting or working closely with research, data science, or product teams

Excellent debugging and problem-solving skills across the full ML stack

Ability to context-switch effectively across multiple concurrent projects

Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to varied audiences

A collaborative mindset with a focus on enabling others to succeed
