Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer (Team Lead) - Various Australian Cities
Ad Text:
Opportunity available:  Head Office in Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne 

At Flight Centre Travel Group (FCTG) our purpose is to 'open up the world for those who want to see'. Every day, we give people all around the world the opportunity to experience something amazing – travel!  

As our Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) Team Leader, you will drive the delivery of high impact, first of their kind AI solutions within the travel industry. You will lead the design and productionisation of advanced AI products that operate reliably at scale, shaping the long-term AI strategy and platform foundations.

This role spans Classical ML, Generative AI, AI agents and agentic workflows. You will define robust ML and LLM infrastructure, establish strong engineering standards across MLOps and LLMOps, and ensure production grade evaluation, safeguards and observability across all AI systems.

If you enjoy coaching and mentoring engineers, building technical depth across the team, and managing multiple concurrent AI initiatives from discovery through to large scale deployment, we encourage you to apply! 

This is a rare opportunity to lead industry defining AI capabilities and deliver advanced, scalable AI products in one of the most data rich sectors globally!

 

Day to Day

 

Lead the development and productionisation of ML models, LLM powered systems and agent based applications
Define and build end to end MLOps including CI CD, model registry, monitoring, drift detection and retraining for predictive ML systems
Establish LLMOps standards including context engineering, automated evaluation pipelines, red teaming, safeguards and policy guardrails
Architect and build AI agent workflows, endpoints, gateways and orchestration layers enabling secure tool access, structured reasoning and multi agent collaboration
Design and govern MCP servers and modern agent communication protocols to ensure interoperability, security and scalability
Implement strong observability across ML and GenAI systems including reliability, latency, evaluation metrics, usage tracking and cost control
Drive scalable ML infrastructure, feature stores and data platforms on Databricks
Oversee Kubernetes based deployments and cloud native AI infrastructure
Partner with senior stakeholders to prioritise and deliver multiple high impact AI initiatives
Coach and grow a high performing AI engineering team

 

We would love to hear from you, if you have:

 

7+ years delivering production grade ML or AI systems with proven commercial impact
3+ years Leading and Mentoring engineers
Experience building AI agents, RAG systems or LLM powered applications in production
Demonstrated experience leading technical teams and managing complex AI programmes
Strong hands on experience across ML infrastructure, distributed systems and scalable AI architecture
Experience building and governing AI agent platforms including endpoints, gateways and tool orchestration
Familiarity with MCP servers and emerging agent communication standards and protocols
Experience defining evaluation frameworks, safety mechanisms and governance for LLM and agent based systems
Deep knowledge of Python, modern AI/ML frameworks and scalable AI platforms including Databricks
Strong expertise in Kubernetes and cloud native production environments

About the Position
This is a hands-on data analyst role focused on delivering Power BI reporting and dashboards across commercial operations for one of Melbourne's most iconic event business. The data environment spans an ERP system alongside specialist event
technology platforms, meaning you'll need to be comfortable working across multiple data sources and translating complex datasets into clear, actionable reporting for commercial and operational stakeholders.

Beyond the immediate deliverables, this role has a clear growth trajectory. As the data team matures, there will be opportunities to move into enterprise-level analytics, including customer segmentation, loyalty program design, and precinct-wide strategic insights. This makes it ideal for someone who wants to be part of building something, not just maintaining it.

 
Job Responsibilities
BI & Reporting

Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports across commercial functions including P&L reporting, event performance analysis, and venue space utilisation.
Connect to and model data from multiple source systems including the organisation's ERP and event technology platforms, ensuring data accuracy and consistency across reports.

Contribute to building a collaborative, high-trust data culture across the organisation.

Technical Skills

Approximately 5 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field.
Strong proficiency in Power BI including report development, data modelling, DAX, and Power Query.
Experience working with ERP data and ideally exposure to event technology platforms.
Solid SQL skills and experience working with multiple data sources to build cohesive reporting solutions.
Understanding of commercial and financial data, particularly P&L structures and performance metrics.

Curious and analytical, someone who naturally wants to dig deeper into the data to find the real story.
Highly collaborative, with the ability to build strong working relationships across functions and seniority levels.
An interest in the events, entertainment, or sports industry and a desire to understand the commercial dynamics of a major precinct.
Comfortable operating in a small, growing team where you'll have genuine ownership and influence over outcomes.
Enterprise-minded — excited by the opportunity to grow into broader customer and strategic analytics over time.

About Us

With a focus on innovation and delivering better customer outcomes, there has never been a better time to work in the Tasmanian energy sector with Aurora Energy. Be a part of Tasmania’s energy future and make a strong contribution to the ongoing success of our business.

We work hard to create a workplace that is welcoming and flexible, while balancing the needs of our people, the business and our customers. Our ‘Better Fit’ Program offers hybrid working after a training and/or probationary period to support work-life balance.

Aurora Energy will invest in you with training and education, celebrate your successes through recognition and reward, and provide a range of employee benefits including an electricity discount, generous leave benefits, and support for your health and wellbeing. 

We believe that every employee has a role to play in contributing to a positive and supportive workplace culture. Our Aurora Way values have been created by our people, for our people. As an employee, you’ll use these values every day to guide your behaviours and actions. Together we’ll continue to make Aurora Energy a great place to work. 

This role sits within our Digital Transformation and Operations Business Unit. They are at the forefront of developing and rolling out new technologies, partnering with the business to solve complex problems, keeping Aurora Energy’s data and systems secure, and much more.   

They collaborate with stakeholders across Aurora Energy to understand problems and identify the best way forward. They take a team-based, people-centric approach. In their words: “There are lots of exciting things to do to support Aurora Energy’s digital transformation, and this breadth is what keeps it interesting.”  

Digital Transformation and Operations is made up of three parts. The AI Lead sits within the Digital Strategy team.

Our Digital Strategy team helps drive our digital transformation and explores opportunities with the business to improve and solve problems. They’re known for working closely with the business and asking a lot of curious questions to make sure that they have the information they need to make the solution fit for purpose. They look after solution architecture and design through to delivery and operation. 

Aurora Energy has a significant focus on investing in digital solutions to support improved outcomes for our people and customers. It is a great time to join the team and be part of this exciting transformation.  

About the Role

As AI Lead, you’ll help shape how Aurora Energy uses artificial intelligence as part of our digital transformation. You’ll work across the business to spot opportunities where AI can make a real difference and help bring those ideas to life in a safe, practical and people‑focused way.

You’ll partner closely with teams across Aurora Energy to unpack complex challenges, ask curious questions, and turn ideas into ethical, fit‑for‑purpose AI solutions. You’ll help guide our AI capability and ways of working, while staying hands‑on where it adds the most value. This is a great opportunity to help build Aurora’s AI maturity and support better outcomes for our people and customers.

About You

You’re curious, collaborative and understand how to use AI to solve meaningful problems. You like working closely with the business, getting to the heart of what’s needed, and turning ideas into practical outcomes.

You’ll have:

Experience working with AI and associated tools
Experience in engaging stakeholders and developing strong relationships
The ability to consult across different business functions to assist with their unique challenges
The ability to explain complex ideas in a clear and engaging way
Experience shaping direction or roadmaps and seeing initiatives through from idea to delivery
A thoughtful approach to responsible and ethical AI, including governance and risk management
A people‑centred mindset, with strong collaboration and influencing skills

A degree in business, strategy or technology discipline or relevant experience is required.

What’s on Offer

With a focus on innovation and delivering better customer outcomes, there has never been a better time to work in the Tasmanian energy sector with Aurora Energy. Be a part of Tasmanians’ energy future and make a strong contribution to the ongoing success of our business.

We work hard to create a workplace that is welcoming and flexible, while balancing the needs of our people, the business and our customers.

Our ‘Better Fit’ Program offers hybrid working after a training and/or probationary period. Our offices feature collaborative spaces, quiet pods, sit/stand desks, and meetings rooms of all sizes—all fully integrated with the latest collaborative working technologies and amenities.

We build and scale advanced solutions—from predictive modeling and personalization to LLM-powered systems, incentive optimization, and causal inference—to unlock step-change growth across the customer lifecycle.

As Senior Product Manager – Institutional, you will work directly with customers, sales, consulting, and operations to identify and validate common needs of the banks, larger institutional brokers, and non-bank market makers that make up our institutional target market.  You’ll translate those needs into actionable production direction in harmony with our overall strategy and collaborate closely with development colleagues to build new and enhanced capabilities to meet customer needs in differentiable ways.  Based in Singapore, Sydney, or Canberra, you'll follow your product enhancements into production to ensure they’re having the desired positive impact and gather new input for further advancement of our solutions.


Key Roles and Responsibilities

Product Strategy & Discovery

Partner with Sales, Relationship Management, and key clients to deeply understand institutional trading workflows and pain points

Work with the Managing Director – Institutional to size market opportunities, organize feature requests into cohesive initiatives, and prioritize within the institutional roadmap

Conduct customer research and discovery—including on-site visits with institutional clients in the APAC region

Define & Deliver

Define business requirements for institutional trading capabilities including:

Responsibilities


About the Team: The TikTok LIVE team’s mission is to build a real-time ecosystem where communities connect through interactive, meaningful experiences. We sit at the intersection of entertainment and technology, working hand-in-hand with the world’s most influential creators and agencies to redefine what "live" means. As the TikTok LIVE Creator Growth Strategist, you won't just manage a list; you will decode the DNA of creator success.

You will own the end-to-end growth methodology for a specific cohort within the ANZ ecosystem. This role is for a "behavioral architect"—someone who can look at a spreadsheet of data, understand the human motivation behind the numbers, and design scalable operational systems that drive revenue, viewership, and creator loyalty. You will be responsible for moving creators from "streaming" to "thriving" by implementing high-impact growth initiatives, gamified incentives, and content best practices tailored to the Australian and

New Zealand markets. Responsibilities:

Behavioral Growth Design: Develop and execute scalable growth frameworks by directly working with selected creators, analyzing creator archetypes, motivations, and the psychological drivers of their performance.
Strategic Execution: Translate creator LIVE behaviors and leverage platform data sets into operational "playbooks" (timing, content formats, feature adoption) that shift creator behavior and drive measurable ROI.
Influence & Monetization: Design and implement "Action Plans"—leveraging incentives, educational programs, and gamification—to accelerate monetization and content quality within your assigned cohort.
Content Incubation: Lead content evaluation for your tier, identifying "what works" to create actionable case studies.

You will design incubation programs that transform raw talent into platform icons.

Community Architecture: Build and scale digital and physical community channels.

You will ensure that communication with creators is not just a broadcast, but a feedback loop that fosters belonging and education.

Metric Ownership: Own the "Health Score" of your cohort, including revenue growth, creator retention, and the "graduation rate" of creators moving to higher tiers.
Qualifications


Minimum Qualifications:

5+ years of experience in creator growth, business development, behavioral strategy, or community scaling.
The "Growth" Mindset: A proven track record of scaling performance across diverse tiers or verticals—you know how to move the needle for 100 creators as effectively as you do for one.
Analytical Depth: Proficiency in translating business problems into analytical solutions.

You don't just report data; you tell a story with it and provide a solution.

Ecosystem Expertise: A deep understanding of the LIVE streaming landscape, the creator lifecycle, and diverse monetization models (e.g., virtual gifting, e-commerce, subscriptions).
Bias for Action: Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple projects without losing sight of the strategic "big picture."

Preferred Qualifications:

Strategic Empathy: High EQ with a natural ability to mentor and coach creators through their unique challenges.
Trend Spotting: Direct experience in mobile entertainment with an intuitive grasp of emerging trends and ANZ-specific user behaviors.
Communication Mastery: Exceptional cross-functional collaboration skills; able to influence internal stakeholders and external creators with equal ease.

Job Profile Summary

The OCM Senior Analyst plays a key role in designing, leading, and delivering organisational change initiatives that enable leaders, teams, and organisations to successfully adopt changes across business processes, systems, technology, and operating models. Working as a trusted member of the Unisys Organisational Change Management (OCM) team, this role partners closely with OCM Managers, project leadership, and senior stakeholders to drive measurable adoption outcomes.

The Senior OCM Analyst brings strong hands‑on experience, sound judgment, and methodological discipline to change delivery—owning workstreams end‑to‑end, coaching junior team members, and ensuring change activities are aligned to business outcomes, risk management, and benefits realisation.

Key Responsibilities

Change Strategy & Delivery

Lead the design and execution of OCM activities aligned to project objectives, business outcomes, and stakeholder needs.

Conduct and interpret change impact, readiness, and risk assessments at individual, team, and organisational levels.

Develop and tailor integrated change strategies, including communications, engagement, training, and resistance management plans.

Proactively identify change risks and adoption barriers; design mitigation strategies to address resistance and capability gaps.

Track, analyse, and report on adoption metrics, behavioural indicators, and change effectiveness to inform decision‑making.

Contribute to sustainment planning to embed change into business‑as‑usual operations.

Translate complex transformation initiatives into clear, actionable messages for diverse audiences.

Communication & Engagement

Lead the development of high‑impact, audience‑specific communications (executive briefings, change narratives, FAQs, campaigns).

Create multimedia and digital collateral (videos, infographics, intranet content) to drive awareness and engagement.

Ensure messaging is consistent, timely, and aligned with overall program governance and brand standards.

Training & Learning Support

Lead training needs analysis and learning strategy development aligned to role‑based capability requirements.

Design and review training materials, learning journeys, and reinforcement mechanisms.

Support and co‑facilitate training sessions for frontline staff, leaders, and executives as required.

Ensure training effectiveness is measured and iterated based on feedback and adoption outcomes.

Collaboration & Project Support

Work autonomously while aligning closely with OCM Managers, Program Leads, and delivery partners.

Facilitate workshops, feedback sessions, and stakeholder briefings to gather insights and refine approaches.

Contribute to OCM practice development, standards, templates, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Support governance activities including status reporting, risk escalation, and executive updates.

Required Skills & Capabilities

Strong written, verbal, and visual communication skills in English; additional languages (e.g., German, Dutch, other European languages) are an advantage.

Proven ability to design and deliver end‑to‑end change management activities across complex initiatives.

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills, including experience working with senior leaders.

Ability to synthesise complex information and data into clear insights and practical recommendations.

Solid working knowledge of change management methodologies (e.g., Prosci / ADKAR, CMBoK) and their practical application.

Creative mindset with interest in using design tools (e.g., Adobe Creative Cloud or similar) for basic graphic or video editing.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (PowerPoint, Word, Excel, SharePoint).

High level of organisation, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable operating in fast‑paced, matrixed environments.

Preferred / Desirable Skills

Experience supporting large‑scale digital transformation, system implementations, or operating model changes.

Background in Business Process Improvement, Knowledge Management, Learning & Development, or Instructional Design.

Familiarity with reporting and dashboard tools (e.g., Power BI, ServiceNow, SharePoint analytics).

Experience leveraging AI‑assisted tools (e.g., Copilot, ChatGPT) to enhance communications, analysis, or engagement.

Exposure to regulated, government, health, or enterprise environments.

Experience contributing to OCM practice maturity, tooling, or capability uplift initiatives.

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Business, Psychology, Organisational Development, Human Resources, or a related field.

2–5 years of relevant experience in Organisational Change Management, transformation, or related roles.

Prosci or equivalent change management certification (or progress toward certification) preferred.

Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered.

6-month Contract with potential extension
Hybrid working arrangement (2-3 days onsite in Eastern Suburbs of Melbourne)
Daily rate: $700 (inc SUPER)

ROLE OVERVIEW
We are seeking a highly analytical IT Business Analyst to drive major technology initiatives. In this role, you will leverage data to prioritize and modernize physical locations requiring critical IT infrastructure upgrades. Additionally, you will assess our broader technology landscape by mapping user lifecycles, evaluating current systems against enterprise architecture, and building strategic roadmaps to optimize the overall experience.

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Conduct evidence-based research and data analysis to evaluate, prioritize, and select physical locations that require immediate IT capability upgrades.
Evaluate the current technology stack and software applications to ensure they align perfectly with the broader business architecture and operational goals.
Map out existing value chains and operational workflows, with a specific focus on defining and optimizing the high-school student lifecycle.
Drive the end-to-end requirements lifecycle, capturing robust business, user, and functional specifications tailored to business capabilities.
Analyze and reverse-engineer existing legacy systems to uncover current technical capabilities and constraints.
Formulate strategic recommendations based on your analysis and confidently present these insights to the senior leadership team.
Actively support the execution of IT projects, bringing strong delivery skills to the table, and assisting the team with scoping and effort estimation for upcoming work.
Take ownership of key project documentation, producing comprehensive Business Analysis Plans, compelling Business Cases, detailed Student Lifecycle models, and day-to-day analysis reports.
Partner closely with the Project Manager to prepare and deliver robust project governance packs.

MORE ABOUT YOU

Possess a highly consultative mindset, allowing you to untangle complex problems and deliver pragmatic solutions within a fast-paced environment.

Exhibit highly effective communication skills, capable of leading clear, productive discussions with both internal teams and external partners.

A proven track record of top-tier stakeholder management, ensuring all project sponsors, technical teams, and business units are kept aligned and informed.

Have a natural ability to build trust and rapport quickly, particularly with local staff and operational personnel.

Deep experience in enterprise analysis, with the unique ability to reverse-engineer technical systems while keeping the focus firmly on business value and user experience.

The role includes mentoring business analysts whilst ensuring effective communication and stakeholder engagement. The focus of this role is to ensure delivered solutions meet business objectives and drive operational efficiency.

Is this opportunity for you?

To perform successfully in this role, you must demonstrate a highly developed ability to manage staff and build client focused and effective teams providing high-quality services to clients in line with contemporary human resource management policies, procedures and practices.

eHealth Queensland is one of the largest Information Communication Technology operations in the state. It is responsible for ensuring the smooth operation of information systems and technologies so that Queensland Health employees and health providers have access to information that supports the delivery of health care.

About Delivery Services Branch - eHealth Queensland:

The Delivery Services Branch is Queensland Health's trusted partner and advisor for digital health project delivery excellence. The branch includes the following units:

Digital Solutions and Technology
Digital Health and Business Solutions
Delivery Support Services
Clinical Program
Digital Partnership Teams
Digitally Enabled Business Solutions
Digital Capital Program

The core function of the branch is to ensure successful delivery of major IT projects and initiatives to our customers across Queensland Health for the benefit of all Queenslanders. This function is critical to eHealth Queensland realising its overall vision, which is to 'advance healthcare through digital innovation'.

As an integral part of our professional project delivery team, each branch team member puts the customer at the centre of everything they do and to actively seek out solutions that will help eHealth Queensland deliver on its targets.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland, you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development
